generatedSentence: Out in the ochlocracy, where elections were a mere drama, a kindly greybeard dealer tracked an uptick in advertising for hexagonal fiber networks. People, common and rowdy, gathered around a campfire, their voting talent disavowed as kestrels circled above the sparse vegetation, sensing the berserk tension and a looming detonation that threatened control over all objects. Despite the majors' obdurate stance, demanding endurance from citizens in newly vacated lavatories lit by dim lumens, the breeding intelligences couldn't ratify such terms. A scarecrow with a distinctive stripe, now wearing a holster, became a focal point for a jamboree, as he practiced jujutsu in the gym, his ankle hurting from a previous jab, knowing the price of freedom. Under ultraviolet daylight, the dramatics began; a large, chunky twinjet outpaced our brief cellular rest, its velocity making the sound of neutrino engines palpable. A nymph, now a majored angler, understood the infinite stakes of this volatile journey. Her coordination, fueled by a flapjack and inner resolve, allowed her to grapple, avoiding an eviscerated future, and kickstart a majorly juvenile resistance. From heaven, a bluejay watched as the humble archer, wearing pyjamas with lockstitches and a leatherbacks motif, gave the signal. The enemy's outdating strategies and clubs were outpaced; their droppings and keyways revealed, their control flipped. The apple of peace, once offended, had a new flavor as the Incas' spirit and electric strength led to their vanquishment, not a doorknock from custody, but freedom.
panelSentences: Out in the ochlocracy, where elections were a mere drama, a kindly greybeard dealer tracked an uptick in advertising for hexagonal fiber networks. People, common and rowdy, gathered around a campfire, their voting talent disavowed as kestrels circled above the sparse vegetation, sensing the berserk tension and a looming detonation that threatened control over all objects.

Despite the majors' obdurate stance, demanding endurance from citizens in newly vacated lavatories lit by dim lumens, the breeding intelligences couldn't ratify such terms. A scarecrow with a distinctive stripe, now wearing a holster, became a focal point for a jamboree, as he practiced jujutsu in the gym, his ankle hurting from a previous jab, knowing the price of freedom.

Under ultraviolet daylight, the dramatics began; a large, chunky twinjet outpaced our brief cellular rest, its velocity making the sound of neutrino engines palpable. A nymph, now a majored angler, understood the infinite stakes of this volatile journey. Her coordination, fueled by a flapjack and inner resolve, allowed her to grapple, avoiding an eviscerated future, and kickstart a majorly juvenile resistance.

From heaven, a bluejay watched as the humble archer, wearing pyjamas with lockstitches and a leatherbacks motif, gave the signal. The enemy's outdating strategies and clubs were outpaced; their droppings and keyways revealed, their control flipped. The apple of peace, once offended, had a new flavor as the Incas' spirit and electric strength led to their vanquishment, not a doorknock from custody, but freedom.
rawPanelSentences: Out in the ochlocracy, where elections were a mere drama, a kindly greybeard dealer tracked an uptick in advertising for hexagonal fiber networks. People, common and rowdy, gathered around a campfire, their voting talent disavowed as kestrels circled above the sparse vegetation, sensing the berserk tension and a looming detonation that threatened control over all objects.

Despite the majors' obdurate stance, demanding endurance from citizens in newly vacated lavatories lit by dim lumens, the breeding intelligences couldn't ratify such terms. A scarecrow with a distinctive stripe, now wearing a holster, became a focal point for a jamboree, as he practiced jujutsu in the gym, his ankle hurting from a previous jab, knowing the price of freedom.

Under ultraviolet daylight, the dramatics began; a large, chunky twinjet outpaced our brief cellular rest, its velocity making the sound of neutrino engines palpable. A nymph, now a majored angler, understood the infinite stakes of this volatile journey. Her coordination, fueled by a flapjack and inner resolve, allowed her to grapple, avoiding an eviscerated future, and kickstart a majorly juvenile resistance.

From heaven, a bluejay watched as the humble archer, wearing pyjamas with lockstitches and a leatherbacks motif, gave the signal. The enemy's outdating strategies and clubs were outpaced; their droppings and keyways revealed, their control flipped. The apple of peace, once offended, had a new flavor as the Incas' spirit and electric strength led to their vanquishment, not a doorknock from custody, but freedom.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined female archer wearing a futuristic, segmented armor with bioluminescent accents and a powerful energy bow. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A vast, dystopian cityscape under a twilight sky, showing people gathered around a small campfire while glowing hexagonal fiber lines crawl up buildings, with a shadowy figure (the dealer) observing from a high window. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A detailed close-up of the protagonist (female archer) in a high-tech gym, her ankle wrapped, performing a graceful jujutsu move against a holographic opponent, with an illuminated holster at her hip. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A dynamic action shot from a low-angle perspective, showing the protagonist mid-air, grappling onto the wing of a sleek, futuristic twinjet as it streaks across a neon-lit sky, explosions in the distance. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A triumphant, wide shot of the protagonist, bow raised, standing on a defeated, glowing robotic antagonist, surrounded by liberated people cheering, with ancient Inca-inspired structures in the background, bathed in electric light.

definitions.definition: A means of exit, escape, reprieve, etc.
definitions.example: They wrote the law to give those organizations an out.

definitions.definition: A state in which a member of the batting team is removed from play due to the application of various rules of the game such as striking out, hitting a fly ball which is caught by the fielding team before bouncing, etc.

A dismissal; a state in which a member of the batting team finishes his turn at bat, due to the application of various rules of the game, such as the bowler knocking over the batsman's wicket with the ball.
A card which can make a hand a winner.
A trip out; an outing.
(chiefly in the plural) One who, or that which, is out; especially, one who is out of office.
A place or space outside of something; a nook or corner; an angle projecting outward; an open space.
A word or words omitted by the compositor in setting up copy; an omission.
To eject; to expel.
To reveal (a person) as LGBT+ (gay, trans, etc).
To reveal (a person or organization) as having a certain secret, such as a being a secret agent or undercover detective.
To reveal (a secret).
definitions.example: A Brazilian company outed the new mobile phone design.

definitions.definition: To come or go out; to get out or away; to become public or apparent.
Not at home; not at one's office or place of employment.
definitions.example: I'm sorry, Mr Smith is out at the moment.

definitions.definition: Not inside or within something.
definitions.example: I worked away cleaning the U-bend until all the gunge was out.

definitions.definition: Freed from confinement or secrecy.
definitions.example: My secret is out.

definitions.definition: Not fitted or inserted into something.
definitions.example: The TV won't work with the plug out!

definitions.definition: Of the ball or other playing implement, falling or passing outside the bounds of the playing area.
definitions.example: I thought the ball hit the line, but the umpire said it was out.

definitions.definition: Released, available for purchase, download or other use.
definitions.example: Did you hear? Their newest CD is out!

definitions.definition: (in various games; used especially of a batsman or batter in cricket or baseball) Dismissed from play under the rules of the game.
definitions.example: He bowls, Johnson pokes at it ... and ... Johnson is out! Caught behind by Ponsonby!

definitions.definition: Openly acknowledging that one is LGBT+ (gay, trans, etc).
definitions.example: It's no big deal to be out in the entertainment business.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Open, public; public about or openly acknowledging some (usually specified) identity.
(of flowers) In bloom.
definitions.example: The garden looks beautiful now that the roses are out.

definitions.definition: (of the sun, moon or stars) Visible in the sky; not obscured by clouds.
definitions.example: The sun is out, and it's a lovely day.

definitions.definition: (of lamps, fires etc.) Not shining or burning.
definitions.example: I called round to the house but all the lights were out and no one was home.

definitions.definition: (of ideas, plans, etc.) Discarded; no longer a possibility.
definitions.example: Right, so that idea's out. Let's move on to the next one.

definitions.definition: (of certain services, devices, or facilities) Not available; out of service.
definitions.example: My wi-fi is out.

definitions.definition: (of a user of a service) Not having availability of a service, as power or communications.
definitions.example: Most of the city got service back yesterday, but my neighborhood is still out.

definitions.definition: (of an organization, etc.) Temporarily not in operation, or not being attended as usual.
definitions.example: when school gets out for today, when college is out for the summer

definitions.definition: Of the tide, at or near its lowest level.
definitions.example: You can walk to the island when the tide's out.

definitions.definition: No longer popular or in fashion.
definitions.example: Black is out this season. The new black is white.

definitions.definition: Without; no longer in possession of; not having more
definitions.example: Do you have any bread? Sorry, we're out.

definitions.definition: (of calculations or measurements) Containing errors or discrepancies; in error by a stated amount.
definitions.example: Nothing adds up in this report. All these figures are out.

definitions.definition: Of a young lady: having entered society and available to be courted.
Away from the inside, centre or other point of reference.
definitions.example: For six hours the tide flows out, then for six hours it flows in.

definitions.definition: Away from home or one's usual place.
definitions.example: Let's eat out tonight

definitions.definition: Outside; not indoors.
definitions.example: Last night we slept out under the stars.

definitions.definition: Away from; at a distance.
definitions.example: Keep out!

definitions.definition: Into a state of non-operation; into non-existence.
definitions.example: I painted out that nasty mark on the wall.

definitions.definition: To the end; completely.
definitions.example: I haven't finished. Hear me out.

definitions.definition: Used to intensify or emphasize.
definitions.example: The place was all decked out for the holidays.

definitions.definition: (of the sun, moon, stars, etc.) So as to be visible in the sky, and not covered by clouds, fog, etc.
definitions.example: The sun came out after the rain, and we saw a rainbow.

definitions.definition: Of a player, so as to be disqualified from playing further by some action of a member of the opposing team (such as being stumped in cricket).
definitions.example: Wilson was bowled out for five runs.

definitions.definition: From from the inside to the outside of; out of.
(procedure word) A radio procedure word meaning that the station is finished with its transmission and does not expect a response.
definitions.example: Destruction. Two T-72s destroyed. Three foot mobiles down. Out.

definitions.definition: Get out; begone; away!
Mob rule; government by the masses; mobocracy.
A process of choosing a leader, members of parliament, councillors or other representatives by popular vote.
definitions.example: How did you vote in (UK also: at) the last election?

definitions.definition: The choice of a leader or representative by popular vote.
definitions.example: The election of John Smith was due to his broad appeal.

definitions.definition: An option that is selected.
definitions.example: W-4 election

definitions.definition: Any conscious choice.
In Calvinism, God's predestination of saints including all of the elect.
Those who are elected.
A single elongated piece of a given material, roughly round in cross-section, often twisted with other fibers to form thread.
definitions.example: The microscope showed a single blue fiber stuck to the sole of the shoe.

definitions.definition: A material in the form of fibers.
definitions.example: The cloth is made from strange, somewhat rough fiber.

definitions.definition: A material whose length is at least 1000 times its width.
definitions.example: Please use polyester fiber for this shirt.

definitions.definition: Dietary fiber.
definitions.example: Fresh vegetables are a good source of fiber

definitions.definition: Moral strength and resolve.
definitions.example: The ordeal was a test of everyone's fiber.

definitions.definition: The preimage of a given point in the range of a map.
definitions.example: Under this map, any two values in the fiber of a given point on the circle differ by 2π

definitions.definition: Said to be of a morphism over a global element: The pullback of the said morphism along the said global element.
A kind of lightweight thread of execution.
Having six edges, or having a cross-section in the form of a hexagon.
definitions.example: Nuts in engineering are generally hexagonal.

definitions.definition: Having three equal axes which cross at 60° angles, and an unequal axis which crosses the others at 90° angle.
To continue over time.
To follow the tracks of.
definitions.example: My uncle spent all day tracking the deer, whose hoofprints were clear in the mud.

definitions.definition: To make tracks on.
To create a musical recording (a track).
definitions.example: Lil Kyle is gonna track with that DJ next week.

definitions.definition: To make sense; to be consistent with known information
Mounted on tracks.
definitions.example: A tank is a tracked vehicle used in the military.

definitions.definition: One who deals in goods, especially automobiles; a middleman.
definitions.example: That used car dealer gave me a great deal on my 1962 rusted-out Volkswagen bug!

definitions.definition: A drug dealer, one who peddles illicit drugs.
A particular type of stock broker or trader.
The person who deals the cards in a card game.
A composition, normally in prose, telling a story and intended to be represented by actors impersonating the characters and speaking the dialogue
Such a work for television, radio or the cinema (usually one that is not a comedy)
Theatrical plays in general
A situation in real life that has the characteristics of such a theatrical play

Rumor, lying or exaggerated reaction to life or online events; melodrama; an angry dispute or scene; a situation made more complicated or worse than it should be; intrigue or spiteful interpersonal maneuvering.
To strongly and solemnly refuse to own or acknowledge; to deny responsibility for, approbation of, and the like.
definitions.example: He was charged with embezzlement, but he disavows the crime.

definitions.definition: To deny; to show the contrary of; to deny legitimacy or achievement of any kind.
definitions.example: Because of her dissatisfaction, she now disavows the merits of fascism.

definitions.definition: A crazed Norse warrior who fought in a frenzy; a berserker.
Injuriously, maniacally, or furiously violent or out of control.
definitions.example: After seeing his sister stabbed to death, he went berserk and attacked the killer like a wild animal.

definitions.definition: Weird; bizarre.
Any of various small falcons of the genus Falco that hover while hunting.
The common kestrel, Falco tinnunculus.
Having a kind personality; kind, warmhearted, sympathetic.
definitions.example: A kindly old man sits on the park bench every afternoon feeding pigeons.

definitions.definition: Favourable, gentle, pleasant, tidy, auspicious, beneficent.
Lawful.
Natural; inherent to the kind or race.
A small increase or upward change in something that has been steady or declining.
A stock market transaction or quote at a price above a preceding one.
To give (especially public) notice of (something); to announce publicly.
To provide information about a person or goods and services to influence others.
definitions.example: For personal needs, advertise on the internet or in a local newspaper.

definitions.definition: To provide public information about (a product, service etc.) in order to attract public awareness and increase sales.
definitions.example: Over the air, they advertise their product on drive-time radio talk shows and TV news shows.

definitions.definition: To notify (someone) of something; to call someone's attention to something.
In gin rummy, to discard a card of one's preferred suit so as to mislead the opponent into thinking you do not want it.
Communication whose purpose is to influence potential customers about products and services.
The industry or profession made up of such communications.
A boisterous person; a brawler.
Loud and disorderly; riotous; boisterous.
Mutual good, shared by more than one.
A tract of land in common ownership; common land.
The people; the community.

The right of taking a profit in the land of another, in common either with the owner or with other persons; so called from the community of interest which arises between the claimant of the right and the owner of the soil, or between the claimants and other commoners entitled to the same right.
To communicate (something).
To converse, talk.
To have sex.
To participate.
To have a joint right with others in common ground.
To board together; to eat at a table in common.
Mutual; shared by more than one.

definitions.definition: Occurring or happening regularly or frequently; usual.

definitions.definition: Found in large numbers or in a large quantity; usual.
definitions.example: It differs from the common blackbird in the size of its beak.

definitions.definition: Simple, ordinary or vulgar.
(grammar) In some languages, particularly Germanic languages, of the gender originating from the coalescence of the masculine and feminine categories of nouns.
(grammar) Of or pertaining to common nouns as opposed to proper nouns.
Vernacular, referring to the name of a kind of plant or animal, i.e., common name vs. scientific name.
Profane; polluted.
Given to lewd habits; prostitute.

A fire at a campground or on a camping trip, often used for cooking, to provide light and heat, to drive away bugs, and as a focal point for sitting around in the evening and talking, telling stories, and singing.
Used as plural of person; a body of human beings considered generally or collectively; a group of two or more persons.

definitions.definition: Persons forming or belonging to a particular group, such as a nation, class, ethnic group, country, family, etc.
A group of persons regarded as being employees, followers, companions or subjects of a ruler.
One's colleagues or employees.
A person's ancestors, relatives or family.

definitions.definition: The mass of a community as distinguished from a special class (elite); the commonalty; the populace; the vulgar; the common crowd; the citizens.
To stock with people or inhabitants; to fill as with people; to populate.
To become populous or populated.
To inhabit; to occupy; to populate.
To interact with people; to socialize.
An individual; usually a human being.

definitions.definition: The physical body of a being seen as distinct from the mind, character, etc.
Any individual or formal organization with standing before the courts.

definitions.definition: The human genitalia; specifically, the penis.
(grammar) A linguistic category used to distinguish between the speaker of an utterance and those to whom or about whom he or she is speaking. See grammatical person.
A shoot or bud of a plant; a polyp or zooid of the compound Hydrozoa, Anthozoa, etc.; also, an individual, in the narrowest sense, among the higher animals.
A thing that has physical existence.
Objective; the goal, end or purpose of something.
(grammar) The noun phrase which is an internal complement of a verb phrase or a prepositional phrase. In a verb phrase with a transitive action verb, it is typically the receiver of the action.
A person or thing toward which an emotion is directed.
definitions.example: Mary Jane had been the object of Peter's affection for years.

definitions.definition: An instantiation of a class or structure.
An element within a category upon which functions operate. Thus, a category consists of a set of element objects and the functions that operate on them.
Sight; show; appearance; aspect.
A marked natural ability or skill.
definitions.example: He has a real talent for drawing.

definitions.definition: A unit of weight and money used in ancient times in Greece, the Roman Empire, and the Middle East.
A desire or inclination for something.
People of talent, viewed collectively; a talented person.
definitions.example: The director searched their talent pool to fill the new opening.

definitions.definition: The men or (especially) women of a place or area, judged by their attractiveness.

An explosion or sudden report made by the near-instantaneous decomposition or combustion of unstable substances. Specifically, combustion that spreads supersonically via shock compression.

An old man.
Any of the members of a group who have been there the longest, often implying experience.

The act or process of vegetating, or growing as a plant does; vegetable growth.
To cast a vote; to assert a formalized choice in an election

definitions.definition: To choose or grant by means of a vote, or by general consent

definitions.example: Voting, in and of itself, is not evil. But when there are only evils to choose from, we need to seek another choice. ― Justin Deschamps

definitions.definition: Having an associated right for the holder to vote as an owner of business.
definitions.example: The cumulative voting preferred stock remains voting until all cumulative dividends are paid.

definitions.definition: Influence or authority over something.

definitions.definition: A separate group or subject in an experiment against which the results are compared where the primary variable is low or non-existent.
The method and means of governing the performance of any apparatus, machine or system, such as a lever, handle or button.
Restraint or ability to contain one's movements or emotions, or self-control.
A security mechanism, policy, or procedure that can counter system attack, reduce risks, and resolve vulnerabilities; a safeguard or countermeasure.

A duplicate book, register, or account, kept to correct or check another account or register.
An interface element that a computer user interacts with, such as a window or a text box.

Any of the physical factors determining the climate of a place, such as latitude, distribution of land and water, altitude, exposure, prevailing winds, permanent high- or low-barometric-pressure areas, ocean currents, mountain barriers, soil, and vegetation.
A construction in which the understood subject of a given predicate is determined by an expression in context. See control.
To exercise influence over; to suggest or dictate the behavior of.
definitions.example: With a simple remote, he could control the toy truck.

definitions.definition: (construed with for) To design (an experiment) so that the effects of one or more variables are reduced or eliminated.
A military rank between captain and lieutenant colonel.
definitions.example: He used to be a major in the army.

definitions.definition: The main area of study of a student working toward a degree at a college or university.
definitions.example: Midway through his second year of college, he still hadn't chosen a major.

definitions.definition: A student at a college or university concentrating on a given area of study.
definitions.example: She is a math major.

definitions.definition: A person of legal age.
The major premise.
A touchdown, or major score.
A large, commercially successful company, especially a record label that is bigger than an indie.
(British slang) An elder brother (especially at a public school).
A large leaf-cutter ant that acts as a soldier, defending the nest.
To harden; to obdure.
Stubbornly persistent, generally in wrongdoing; refusing to reform or repent.
Physically hardened, toughened.
Hardened against feeling; hard-hearted.
The measure of a person's stamina or persistence.

definitions.definition: Ability to endure hardship.
The length of time that a ship's rations will supply
A vessel or fixture for washing, particularly:
Handwashing, particularly
A liquid used in washing; a lotion; a wash; a rinse.
A washroom: a room used for washing the face and hands.
A room containing a toilet: a bathroom (US) or WC (UK).
A plumbing fixture for urination and defecation: a toilet.
A place to wash clothes: a laundry.
A place where gold is panned.
A paved room in a mortuary where corpses are kept under a shower of disinfecting fluid.
In the International System of Units, the derived unit of luminous flux; the light that is emitted in a solid angle of one steradian from a source of one candela. Symbol: lm.

The cavity bounded by a plant cell wall.
The bore of a tube such as a hollow needle or catheter.
In a thick manner.
Propagation of offspring through sexual reproduction.
The act of insemination by natural or artificial means.

Nurture; education; formation of manners.
Descent; pedigree; extraction.

definitions.definition: (usually with "up") To get angry.
To rise high above, tower above.

definitions.example: We had only two hours to work before daylight.

definitions.definition: Exposure to public scrutiny.
definitions.example: Budgeting a spy organization can't very well be done in daylight.

definitions.definition: A clear, open space.
definitions.example: All small running backs instinctively run to daylight.

definitions.definition: (machinery) The space between platens on a press or similar machinery.
definitions.example: The minimum and maximum daylights on an injection molding machine determines the sizes of the items it can make.

definitions.definition: Emotional or psychological distance between people, or disagreement.
definitions.example: We completely agree. There's no daylight between us on the issue.

definitions.definition: To expose to daylight
To provide sources of natural illumination such as skylights or windows.
To allow light in, as by opening drapes.
(landscaping) To run a drainage pipe to an opening from which its contents can drain away naturally.
To gain exposure to the open.
definitions.example: The seam of coal daylighted at a cliff by the river.

definitions.definition: (used with a singular or plural verb) the art of acting and stagecraft.
(used with a singular or plural verb) dramatic behaviour.
The skeletal joint which connects the foot with the leg; the uppermost portion of the foot and lowermost portion of the leg, which contain this skeletal joint.
To walk.
To cyclically angle the foot at the ankle while pedaling, to maximize the amount of work applied to the pedal during each revolution.
To disembowel, to remove the viscera.
To destroy or make ineffectual or meaningless.
To elicit the essence of.
To remove a bodily organ or its contents.
(of viscera) To protrude through a surgical incision.
A piece of wood or other material, usually long and slender, pointed at one end so as to be easily driven into the ground as a marker or a support or stay.
definitions.example: We have surveyor's stakes at all four corners of this field, to mark exactly its borders.

definitions.definition: A piece of wood driven in the ground, placed in the middle of the court, that is used as the finishing point after scoring 12 hoops in croquet.
A stick inserted upright in a lop, eye, or mortise, at the side or end of a cart, flat car, flatbed trailer, or the like, to prevent goods from falling off.
(with definite article) The piece of timber to which a person condemned to death was affixed to be burned.

definitions.definition: A share or interest in a business or a given situation.

definitions.definition: That which is laid down as a wager; that which is staked or hazarded; a pledge.

A small anvil usually furnished with a tang to enter a hole in a bench top, as used by tinsmiths, blacksmiths, etc., for light work, punching hole in or cutting a work piece, or for specific forming techniques etc.
A territorial division comprising all the Mormons (typically several thousand) in a geographical area.
To fasten, support, defend, or delineate with stakes.

definitions.definition: To pierce or wound with a stake.
To put at risk upon success in competition, or upon a future contingency.
To provide another with money in order to engage in an activity as betting or a business venture.

definitions.definition: The money wagered in gambling.
Risks.
The sky, specifically:
The abode of God or the gods, traditionally conceived as beyond the sky; especially:
The afterlife of the blessed dead, traditionally conceived as opposed to an afterlife of the wicked and unjust (compare hell); specifically:
(by extension) Any paradise; any blissful place or experience.
(by extension) A state of bliss; a peaceful ecstasy.
(with a modifier) Similarly blissful afterlives, places, or states for particular people, animals, or objects.
To transport to the abode of God, the gods, or the blessed.
To beatify, enchant, or please greatly.
To beautify, to make into a paradise.
A delay.

definitions.definition: One who watches; a watchman.
(in the plural) Hautboys, or oboes, played by town musicians.
(in the plural) Musicians who sing or play at night or in the early morning, especially at Christmas time; serenaders; musical watchmen. [formerly waites, wayghtes.]
To delay movement or action until the arrival or occurrence of; to await. (Now generally superseded by “wait for”.)
To delay movement or action until some event or time; to remain neglected or in readiness.

definitions.definition: To wait tables; to serve customers in a restaurant or other eating establishment.

definitions.definition: To attend on; to accompany; especially, to attend with ceremony or respect.
To attend as a consequence; to follow upon; to accompany.

A metal bar on a motorcycle used to start its engine.
An act of starting a motorcycle by quickly depressing the kick start with one's foot.

definitions.definition: An impetus that starts or restarts some process.
To start a motorcycle.

definitions.definition: To start or begin strongly or quickly.

definitions.definition: To make (something) active, functional, or productive again; to reinvigorate (an activity, system, or process).

Of, relating to, consisting of, or resembling a cell or cells.

Liberality, generosity.

definitions.definition: Abundant; ample.
Full in statement; diffuse; profuse.
Free; unencumbered.
Unrestrained by decorum; said of language.

Before the wind.
Something dropped.

definitions.example: to grapple with one's conscience

definitions.definition: (usually with definite article) Electricity; the electricity supply.
definitions.example: We had to sit in the dark because the electric was cut off.

definitions.definition: An electric car.
An electric toothbrush.
A substance or object which can be electrified; an insulator or non-conductor, like amber or glass.
Fencing with the use of a body wire, box, and related equipment to detect when a weapon has touched an opponent.
Of, relating to, produced by, operated with, or utilising electricity; electrical.
Of or relating to an electronic version of a musical instrument that has an acoustic equivalent.
Being emotionally thrilling; electrifying.
